description: Complete bytecode encoding tables for translating NASM assembly into machine code without an external assembler. Covers all instructions generated by the SASM translator for x86-32 and x86-64 targets.
source_references: Intel 64 and IA-32 Architectures Software Developer Manual, Vol. 2

encoding_types.MR: ModRM:reg = source register, ModRM:r/m = destination (register or memory)
encoding_types.RM: ModRM:reg = destination register, ModRM:r/m = source (register or memory)
encoding_types.MI: ModRM:r/m = destination, immediate value follows opcode+ModRM
encoding_types.M: ModRM:r/m = single operand, ModRM:reg field holds opcode extension (/digit)
encoding_types.OI: Register encoded in low 3 bits of opcode byte, immediate follows
encoding_types.O: Register encoded in low 3 bits of opcode byte, no immediate
encoding_types.I: Immediate operand only (e.g., INT imm8, PUSH imm8)
encoding_types.ZO: Zero operands — fixed opcode, no ModRM, no immediate
encoding_types.FD: AL/AX/EAX/RAX ← moffs (direct memory offset, no ModRM)
encoding_types.TD: moffs ← AL/AX/EAX/RAX (direct memory offset, no ModRM)
encoding_types.AI: Accumulator (AL/AX/EAX) + immediate — short form, no ModRM

operand_notation.r8: 8-bit general-purpose register (AL, CL, DL, BL, AH, CH, DH, BH; or SPL, BPL, SIL, DIL with REX)
operand_notation.r16: 16-bit general-purpose register (AX, CX, DX, BX, SP, BP, SI, DI)
operand_notation.r32: 32-bit general-purpose register (EAX, ECX, EDX, EBX, ESP, EBP, ESI, EDI)
operand_notation.Sreg: Segment register (ES, CS, SS, DS, FS, GS)

AH.note: Not accessible when REX prefix is used

CH.note: Not accessible when REX prefix is used

DH.note: Not accessible when REX prefix is used

BH.note: Not accessible when REX prefix is used

SPL.note: Requires any REX prefix to access (replaces AH)

BPL.note: Requires any REX prefix to access (replaces CH)

SIL.note: Requires any REX prefix to access (replaces DH)

DIL.note: Requires any REX prefix to access (replaces BH)

modrm_encoding.description: ModRM byte layout: [mod(2 bits) | reg/opcode(3 bits) | r/m(3 bits)]. The mod field and r/m field together specify the addressing mode. The reg field holds either a register operand or an opcode extension (/digit).
modrm_encoding.byte_layout: bits 7-6: mod | bits 5-3: reg/opcode | bits 2-0: r/m

exceptions.rm_100: SIB byte follows (ESP/RSP encoding is escape to SIB)

sib_encoding.description: SIB (Scale-Index-Base) byte layout: [scale(2 bits) | index(3 bits) | base(3 bits)]. Used when ModRM r/m = 100 (ESP/RSP encoding). Effective address = base + (index * scale) + displacement.
sib_encoding.byte_layout: bits 7-6: scale | bits 5-3: index | bits 2-0: base

special_cases.index_100: No index register (index = ESP/RSP is escape meaning 'none')
special_cases.base_101_mod00: No base register; disp32 only. Address = (index * scale) + disp32

operand_size_override.description: Switches default operand size: in 32/64-bit mode selects 16-bit operands; in 16-bit mode selects 32-bit operands.

address_size_override.description: Switches default address size: in 32-bit mode uses 16-bit addresses; in 64-bit mode uses 32-bit addresses.

rex.description: REX prefix (64-bit mode only). Byte range 0x40-0x4F. Bit layout: 0100WRXB.

R.description: Extends ModRM reg field to access R8-R15

X.description: Extends SIB index field to access R8-R15

B.description: Extends ModRM r/m or SIB base field, or opcode reg field

common_forms.REX.B: 0x41 — extend r/m or base to R8-R15

forms.note: Register code added to 0xB0: AL=0xB0, CL=0xB1, DL=0xB2, BL=0xB3, AH=0xB4, CH=0xB5, DH=0xB6, BH=0xB7
MOV r64, imm64 is the ONLY instruction that can take a full 64-bit immediate
imm32 is sign-extended to 64 bits. For full 64-bit immediate, use MOV r64, imm64 form.
Sign-extend 32-bit to 64-bit. Opcode 0x63 was ARPL in 32-bit mode.
Default 64-bit operand size in 64-bit mode; no REX.W needed
In 64-bit mode, PUSHF defaults to 64-bit push (PUSHFQ)
Short encoding 0x40-0x47 unavailable in 64-bit mode (used as REX prefix)
Short jump: signed 8-bit offset from end of instruction
Near return and pop imm16 bytes from stack
Far return and pop imm16 bytes
Jump if CX=0 (16-bit address-size override in 32-bit mode)
Sign-extend AL to AX (requires 0x66 prefix in 32-bit mode)
Sign-extend AX to EAX (default in 32-bit mode)

section_directives.description: Uninitialized data section (zero-filled at load time)
